description:
Discovered while landing fz-ul4.23.6.3. AOT-compiled binaries hit \"frame_sizes has no entry for fn_id N\" when running fixtures that call fz_alloc_frame_dyn — closures and tail-call-closures. The JIT path populates Process.frame_sizes from CompiledModule at make_process() time; the AOT shim's fz_aot_run does not.

Working in AOT today: add1, hello, cold_fn, tail_recursion, polymorphic.
Failing: apply2, higher_order (and any other closure-heavy fixture).

## Fix

Two viable approaches:

A. Emit a per-program `fz_aot_frame_sizes()` extern fn that returns a static array of (fn_id, size) pairs. fz_aot_run copies them into Process.frame_sizes after Process::new.

B. Emit the frame_sizes table as a global static const in the object, with a known symbol fz_aot_frame_sizes_data. fz_aot_run reads it at startup.

(A) keeps the artifact API simple (one more fn-pointer arg to fz_aot_run); (B) keeps codegen simpler but requires emitting global data via cranelift_object::ObjectModule.

## Acceptance

- apply2.fz / higher_order.fz built via `fz build` runs cleanly and matches JIT/interp output.
- A test_aot_closure unit test in src/ir_codegen.rs or in tests/ proves it.
title: AOT: populate Process.frame_sizes (closures via fz_alloc_frame_dyn)
